what_it_does: This file is a minimal channel-attachment record: YAML frontmatter declaring an attachment id, its channel, an attachment type of images, a target URI pointing to a specific image asset, and an added timestamp.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no body text
standouts: References a specific image asset via an image:// URI-like target
Includes a parenthetical numeric index (46) in the target
Serves as a typed, timestamped attachment ledger entry
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of metadata fields
File name follows an att-<uuid>.md pattern
Target uses an image:// scheme with a channel path
No prose body, only structured metadata
concerns: Target string contains a space and unescaped parentheses, which may complicate parsing
No alt text, caption, or descriptive label for the image
No author or provenance beyond a timestamp
No version or checksum for the referenced asset
notes: A bare attachment stub that links an identifier to a numbered image asset in the design-assets channel.
questions: What does the parenthetical (46) index represent in the target URI?
What is the actual file format, size, or dimensions of the referenced image?
Was this record generated automatically or added manually?
Who added the attachment and what context is it attached to?
intention_signals: It appears intended to record and make retrievable a specific image attachment within the design-assets channel, acting as a pointer from an attachment id to an underlying image asset.
novelty: The target URI embeds a parenthetical numeric index (46) inside an image:// reference, which is a slightly unusual addressing convention.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed link between an attachment identifier and a specific image asset in the design-assets channel, useful for retrieval and provenance in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record (att-6e4547fe) in the design-assets channel that declares an images attachment pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (46) and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to catalog and reference a specific image asset within the design-assets channel.
